title: Communicate Meaning beyond Color
summary: Build and verify css color not only in service status and validation grayscale audit through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css color not only from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css color not only without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Service status and validation grayscale audit case 1: adding a second shade is enough to avoid a color-only distinction.
Service status and validation grayscale audit case 1: pair every meaningful color difference with text, structure, shape, pattern, or programmatic state and verify the task without author colors.
Service status and validation grayscale audit case 1: use red and orange together because two hues count as two information channels.

steps.title: 02 · Read the service status and validation grayscale audit case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 2: Which claim preserves css color not only when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: service status and validation grayscale audit.

content.text: Color can reinforce meaning but must not be the only channel. Text, icons with accessible names where needed, shape, pattern, position, border style, and programmatic state can distinguish status and selection. A second shade is still color-only. Grayscale, color-vision simulations, forced colors, print, and spoken output reveal whether meaning survives. Apply it to service status and validation grayscale audit.
